by_judge.judge: D K SINGH & RAJESH RAI K & T.M.NADAF

narrative:
On June 22, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 49.1%, addressing 1,450 cases out of 2,956 scheduled across 47 judges. This represents a marginal decline in efficiency from the 49.9% hearing rate (1,125 of 2,256) observed on June 19, but a substantial increase in total processed case volume. The court maintained higher activity levels compared to June 17 (43.5%) and June 18 (44.0%) despite managing a significantly heavier roster.

Individual judge outputs varied widely under these intense schedules. Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 successfully heard 155 out of 250 cases (62.0%) in his primary block, alongside 36 of 70 (51.4%) in a supplementary listing. In Hall 19, Justice S Vishwajith Shetty faced the day’s highest block load of 255 cases, converting 98 (38.4%) into active hearings. Justice H.P. Sandesh showcased strong clearance, resolving 64 of 98 matters (65.3%) and completing all 21 cases (100.0%) on his secondary list. Conversely, no hearings proceeded for the 88 cases scheduled under Justice P Sree Sudha.

In terms of hearing stages, Admission and Orders led conversion efficiency, achieving hearing rates of 52.6% (254 of 483) and 50.5% (448 of 888), while Final Hearings lagged at 29.8% (142 of 476). By case type, Writ Petitions dominated the docket with 1,326 scheduled and 612 heard (46.2%). Criminal Petitions registered a conversion rate of 56.5% (268 of 474), whereas Miscellaneous First Appeals remained lower at 35.0% (114 of 326). Criminal Revision Petitions demonstrated exceptional progress, converting 81.1% (73 of 90) of scheduled cases.
